Brown Spot.treatment: Apply Mancozeb or Propiconazole fungicide at recommended dosage. Remove and destroy heavily infected leaves to prevent spore spread.
preventive_actions: Improve field drainage to reduce humidity
Use disease-resistant varieties in next season

Leaf Blast.treatment: Apply Tricyclazole or Isoprothiolane fungicide immediately. Blast spreads rapidly — act within 24 hours of detection.

Neck Blast.treatment: Apply Tricyclazole at panicle initiation stage. This is a critical stage — neck blast can cause complete yield loss.
preventive_actions: Apply preventive fungicide at boot stage
Neck Blast.chemical: Tricyclazole 75% WP @ 0.6g/L at boot and heading stages

Bacterial Leaf Blight.treatment: Apply Copper Oxychloride or Streptocycline. Drain excess water from fields to reduce bacterial spread.
preventive_actions: Avoid excess nitrogen during vegetative stage

Sheath Blight.treatment: Apply Hexaconazole or Validamycin fungicide. Focus on the lower sheath region near the waterline.
preventive_actions: Reduce planting density to improve air circulation

Early Blight.treatment: Apply Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb fungicide. Remove lower infected leaves. Ensure good air circulation around plants.
preventive_actions: Apply mulch to reduce soil splash

Late Blight.treatment: Apply Metalaxyl + Mancozeb immediately. Late blight is aggressive — destroy severely infected plants to prevent spread.
preventive_actions: Apply protective fungicide before monsoon season

Powdery Mildew.treatment: Apply Sulphur-based fungicide or Triadimefon. Clean infected areas thoroughly before spraying.
preventive_actions: Spray Neem oil as preventive measure
Powdery Mildew.chemical: Wettable Sulphur 80% WP @ 3g/L or Triadimefon 25% WP @ 1g/L

Downy Mildew.treatment: Apply Cymoxanil + Mancozeb or Metalaxyl. Downy mildew thrives in cool, wet conditions — prioritize drainage.
preventive_actions: Apply preventive copper spray before rains

Anthracnose.treatment: Apply Carbendazim or Thiophanate-methyl fungicide. Remove mummified fruits and infected plant debris.
preventive_actions: Harvest at optimal maturity to reduce infection
Avoid injury to fruits during harvesting
Use hot water seed treatment before planting

Rust.treatment: Apply Propiconazole or Tebuconazole fungicide. Rust spreads through wind — act quickly before spore release.
preventive_actions: Scout fields regularly during cool, wet periods

Wilt.treatment: Apply Carbendazim drenching at the base of plants. Wilted plants may not recover — remove and destroy severely affected ones.
preventive_actions: Practice crop rotation with non-host crops
Avoid waterlogging to prevent soil pathogen buildup
Wilt.chemical: Carbendazim 50% WP @ 2g/L soil drench at base

Yellow Mosaic Virus.treatment: No direct cure exists. Remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Control whitefly vectors with Imidacloprid.
preventive_actions: Control whitefly population using sticky traps
Plant barrier crops like sorghum around the field

Stem Rot.treatment: Apply Carbendazim or Propiconazole. Drain waterlogged fields immediately as stem rot is favored by standing water.
preventive_actions: Use silicon-based fertilizers to strengthen stem tissue

Leaf Scorch.treatment: Apply foliar micronutrient spray (Zinc + Boron). Leaf scorch is often linked to nutritional stress or drought.
preventive_actions: Mulch around plant base to retain moisture
Leaf Scorch.chemical: Zinc Sulphate @ 0.5% + Boric acid @ 0.1% foliar spray

default.treatment: Consult local agricultural extension officer for precise diagnosis. Apply broad-spectrum fungicide as precautionary measure.
preventive_actions: Isolate affected plants from healthy crops
Improve general crop hygiene and sanitation
default.chemical: Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2g/L as broad-spectrum protective fungicide
